data.title: RAVE Experiences Significant Liquidations Amid Price Volatility
data.description: RAVE has seen substantial liquidations in the past 24 hours, according to Coinglass data. ChainCatcher reports that the total liquidation amount reached $50.85 million, with long positions accounting for $27.53 million and short positions for $23.32 million. The largest single liquidation was $105,352. The seven-day average liquidation volume is at an extreme level, and RAVE's price has fluctuated by over 100.87% today. Currently, 60,988 individuals have been liquidated globally.

data.title: Multiple Protocols Suspend LayerZero OFT Cross-Chain Bridge Following Attack
data.description:
More than 15 protocols have temporarily suspended the LayerZero OFT cross-chain bridge function as a precautionary measure following an attack on Kelp DAO's rsETH cross-chain bridge, which resulted in losses of approximately $2.92 billion. According to Foresight News, the affected protocols include Ethena, TRON DAO, ApeCoin/ApeChain, ether.fi, mETH Protocol, Solv Protocol, MOCA Foundation, River, f(x) Protocol, Re, cap, Agora, Avant, Flare, and Euler Labs. These protocols have emphasized that they have no direct exposure to rsETH and that user assets remain secure. The suspension is expected to be temporary.

In addition, protocols such as Aave V3/V4, SparkLend, Lido (earnETH), and Fluid have implemented measures to freeze rsETH-related markets or vaults as part of chain risk control, without suspending bridge functions. The protocols are actively monitoring the situation and advise users with exposure to rsETH or LayerZero to stay updated with official project announcements.

data.title: Sberbank Prepares for Cryptocurrency Trading Pending Regulatory Framework
data.description: Sberbank has announced its readiness to provide cryptocurrency trading services, contingent upon the establishment of regulatory measures and organized exchange trading. According to NS3.AI, Senior Vice President Ruslan Vesterovsky emphasized that exchange-based trading could enhance liquidity and narrow spreads. The bank's move signals a proactive approach to entering the cryptocurrency market, aligning with potential regulatory developments.

data.title: Aave Faces Significant Sell-Off Amid KelpDAO Vulnerability
data.description: Aave experienced a notable decline of over 18% today following a vulnerability in KelpDAO, prompting several major investors to sell off their holdings. According to PANews, the monitoring service Lookonchain reported that prominent investors have been offloading AAVE tokens. One investor, smaugvision, sold 20,015 AAVE at an average price of $103 per token, receiving 2.06 million USDC in return. Another major investor, identified as 0xFC56, sold 20,000 AAVE at the same average price, exchanging them for 2.05 million USDC.

data.title: Charles Schwab and Citadel Securities Explore Prediction Markets
data.description:
Traditional finance leaders Charles Schwab and Citadel Securities are contemplating entry into the burgeoning prediction markets sector. According to Cointelegraph, Charles Schwab CEO Rick Wurster expressed interest in prediction markets during a call with investors, noting that while the concept hasn't garnered significant interest from Schwab clients, the company is considering a thorough evaluation of the opportunity. Wurster emphasized that Schwab's potential involvement would focus on long-term wealth-building rather than areas like sports, politics, or pop culture, which are not aligned with the company's goals. He highlighted the poor success rates of gamblers as a reason to avoid such markets.

Prediction markets, including platforms like Kalshi and Polymarket, have seen a surge in activity, with a record monthly trading volume of $23.6 billion in March, as reported by Token Terminal. However, these platforms have faced scrutiny from U.S. state regulators for allegedly offering unlicensed sports betting. Federal lawmakers have also criticized them for insufficient measures against insider trading. Meanwhile, Citadel Securities President Jim Esposito, speaking at a Semafor conference in Washington, D.C., indicated that the company is monitoring developments in prediction markets. Although Citadel is not currently involved due to liquidity concerns, Esposito acknowledged the potential for market growth and expressed interest in event contracts as a risk management tool for retail and institutional clients. He noted that contracts related to elections could serve as a hedge against investment risks, providing a strategic advantage for investors. Esposito emphasized the importance of having a clear method to manage certain risks, suggesting a viable industrial logic for such contracts.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Discusses Hormuz Strait Situation with Pakistan Army Chief
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump recently engaged in discussions with Pakistan's Army Chief, Munir, focusing on the situation in the Hormuz Strait and the details of agreements with Iran. According to BlockBeats, the talks aimed to address regional security concerns and diplomatic relations.

data.title: US media: Trump appears arrogant in public, but is filled with fear inside.
data.description: The Wall Street Journal reports that US President Trump seems to have lost his "adventure spirit," while his fears are growing. Previously, in the nearly empty West Wing of the White House, shortly after learning that a US warplane had been shot down in Iran and two pilots were missing, Trump yelled at his aides for hours. He repeatedly said, "The Europeans didn't help at all." According to people who spoke with him, the images of the 1979 Iranian hostage crisis have been haunting him. After the US pilots were rescued, the arrogant president went all in again, attempting to weaken Iran's most powerful bargaining chip—control of the Strait of Hormuz. At the time, Trump tweeted, "Open the Strait, you lunatics." This president, who has always been keen on creating dramatic scenes, applied his unorthodox and extreme style to a war in an even more extreme way. He sometimes adopts a belligerent stance, sometimes a conciliatory strategy, but at the same time, he is agonizing behind the scenes about how far the situation could escalate. He feared that ordering troops into dangerous areas would result in injuries and prevent some from returning home, a situation similar to that of other presidents who had experienced war. (Jin Shi)

data.title: Planet Midday News
data.description: 1. ASTEROID's market capitalization surpasses a new high of $170 million; 2. Iran's Deputy Foreign Minister: Iran will never hand over enriched uranium to the United States; 3. Kelp: Has suspended rsETH contracts on its mainnet and multiple L2 platforms and launched an investigation into the attack; 4. RAVE falls below $1, down over 96% intraday; 5. Spark's Head of Strategy: The rsETH security incident has exacerbated the spillover risks, and the DeFi market may face a cascading liquidation crisis; 6. Aave's TVL drops to $21.966 billion, down 16.78% in a single day; 7. etherFi: Has suspended LayerZero bridging and frozen multiple asset deposits and withdrawals, but has not yet directly exposed rsETH risks; 8. Curve's founder: The KelpDAO attack caused bad debts on Aave, exposing the risks of non-segregated lending; 9. Lido: Due to the Kelp security incident, earnETH deposits have been suspended, stETH and the staking protocol are unaffected; 10. Andre Cronje: Aave has no subsidy mechanism for user losses and has already... ETH was withdrawn to the fund management encapsulation contract; 11. The founder of ACI terminated the Frontier staking program and transferred ETH to Aave DAO for risk management.

data.title: U.S. President Trump Faces Growing Fear Amid Iran Tensions
data.description:
On April 19, the Wall Street Journal reported that U.S. President Donald Trump appears to have lost his 'adventurous spirit,' with his fears increasing daily. According to BlockBeats, Trump was informed in the nearly empty West Wing of the White House that a U.S. fighter jet had been shot down in Iran, with two pilots missing. He reportedly spent hours expressing frustration to his aides, repeatedly stating, 'The Europeans haven't helped at all.' People familiar with his thoughts revealed that images of the 1979 Iran hostage crisis lingered in his mind. After the American pilots were rescued, the confident president took a bold step to weaken Iran's strongest leverage—control over the Strait of Hormuz.

Trump tweeted, 'Open the strait, you lunatics.' Known for his dramatic flair, the president applied his unconventional and extreme approach to the conflict in an even more radical manner. He alternated between aggressive postures and conciliatory strategies, while privately contemplating the potential escalation of the situation. He feared ordering troops into dangerous areas, which could result in injuries and prevent some from returning home, a concern shared by other presidents who have experienced war.

data.title: DeFi Security Alert as Attackers Exploit Fake Messages
data.description: An attack exploiting fake messages has led to the depletion of mainnet reserves, resulting in the invalidation of rsETH collateral. According to PANews, this incident has caused significant bad debt for lending protocols such as Aave. The event serves as a stark reminder of the ongoing security challenges facing the decentralized finance (DeFi) sector.

data.title: Uniswap Blocks IP Range Due to DDoS-Level Access
data.description: Uniswap's governance page has become inaccessible due to a large-scale automated access from certain Tencent Cloud infrastructure IP ranges. According to Foresight News, this activity reached the level of a DDoS attack, prompting Uniswap to block the affected IP ranges to maintain page stability. VPN users utilizing the same IP range are advised to switch to other regions or non-Tencent Cloud nodes to regain access. The identity and motive of the attackers remain unknown.

data.title: USDT Borrowing and Deposit Rates Surge on Aave Platform
data.description: On April 19, data from the Aave platform indicated a significant increase in the annual percentage yield (APY) for USDT borrowing, reaching 14.99%, while the deposit APY surged to 13.39%. According to BlockBeats, this spike in rates is linked to a security incident involving KelpDAO. The hacker used Aave to collateralize illegally minted rsETH and borrowed a substantial amount of ETH. In response to this breach, over $54 billion in assets were urgently withdrawn from Aave as a precautionary measure. This mass withdrawal of funds is believed to have contributed to the sharp rise in USDT borrowing and deposit rates on the platform.

data.title: ASTEROID's market capitalization has surpassed a new high of $170 million, currently trading at $166 million, a daily increase of over 900%.
data.description: According to GMGN data, the market capitalization of the Ethereum blockchain's Meme coin ASTEROID has surpassed a new high of $170 million, currently trading at $166 million, representing a daily increase of over 900%. Odaily reminds users that Meme coin prices are highly volatile, and investors should be aware of the risks.

data.title: AI TRENDS | OpenClaw Access to X API Introduced with Cost Adjustments
data.description:
Elon Musk has announced that users can now access the X API through OpenClaw, aiming to reduce usage costs while ensuring sustainable operations. According to Foresight News, the X API team has released a new version of the CLI tool, integrating SKILL.md support, allowing both OpenClaw and Hermes to access the X API.

Since April 20, the X platform has adjusted its API pricing. The cost for Owned Reads has been significantly reduced to $0.001 per request, covering 12 common endpoints such as retrieving tweets, bookmarks, followers, likes, and lists. Meanwhile, the fee for regular posts has increased from $0.01 to $0.015, and posts containing URLs are priced at $0.20. Additionally, features like following, liking, and quote retweeting will be removed from all self-service tiers.

data.title: RAVE fell below $1, down over 96% intraday.
data.description: OKX data shows that RAVE briefly fell below $1, currently trading at $0.98, a drop of over 96% on the day.

data.title: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister: We will never hand over enriched uranium to the United States.
data.description: Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Saeed Khatibzadeh said on the 18th that Iran would never hand over its enriched uranium to the United States. According to Iranian media reports, Khatibzadeh refuted previous statements by US President Trump regarding uranium enrichment. He said Iran is willing to communicate on existing concerns, but will never accept unrealistic preconditions, and "no enriched material will be shipped to the United States." Khatibzadeh said that because the United States refuses to abandon its "maximum pressure" stance on key issues, Iran is not yet ready to hold a new round of face-to-face negotiations with the US. (Xinhua)

data.title: Liquidity Concerns in Stablecoin Market Pose Risks to DeFi Ecosystem
data.description:
Spark Protocol's strategy head, monetsupply.eth, expressed concerns on the X platform about tightening liquidity in the stablecoin market. According to Odaily, the current rsETH security incident may be entering a more dangerous phase, with approximately 16.5% of the ETH market supported by rsETH. If losses are evenly distributed across the mainnet and cross-chain environments, rsETH collateral loans in eMode could face a 10%-15% discount. After risk buffers are exhausted, ETH depositors might still bear a 2%-3% residual loss. This expectation is prompting ETH providers to exit quickly, locking market utilization at 100%, while borrowing rates fail to incentivize the unwinding of leveraged positions in wstETH, weETH, and similar assets to release liquidity.

Additionally, users borrowing stablecoins like USDT against ETH collateral are struggling to close positions promptly, even as stablecoin lending rates rise, disrupting the market's original incentive mechanisms. Monetsupply.eth further noted that in a "locked" state of 100% utilization, the DeFi market could face a cascading liquidation crisis with two major distorted incentives: ETH holders cannot adjust healthy collateral ratios, and liquidators cannot withdraw and sell collateral assets, potentially leading to rapid bad debt accumulation if ETH prices fall. Meanwhile, stablecoin depositors might be motivated to "exit indirectly" by lending other stablecoins, securing about 75% of fund recovery at a lower cost while still yielding positive returns.

For lending markets reliant on liquidity pools and rehypothecation, liquidity must be prioritized. However, Aave's recent reduction of the maximum borrowing rate cap (slope2) is weakening deleveraging incentives, significantly increasing the risk of systemic failures in the market.

data.title: Hong Kong Sees Increased Interest from Middle Eastern Investors
data.description:
Hong Kong is experiencing a rise in interest from Middle Eastern investors, according to ChainCatcher. Hong Kong's Deputy Secretary for Financial Services and the Treasury, Joseph Chan, confirmed that there has been an increase in inquiries about transferring funds to Hong Kong or opening accounts there. He noted that the relationship between Hong Kong and the Middle East is mutually beneficial.

Chan revealed that a licensed virtual insurance company from Hong Kong has expanded its operations to Saudi Arabia and the United Arab Emirates. Additionally, Hong Kong's tokenized funds have been introduced to Middle Eastern wealth management platforms, and Asian investors can purchase Islamic bond ETFs in Hong Kong, providing a familiar and reliable market for Middle Eastern investors.

Chan emphasized the Hong Kong government's commitment to advancing fintech and digital assets. The government is currently formulating legislative proposals for licensing digital asset trading and custody services, aiming to establish a comprehensive regulatory framework and position Hong Kong as a global hub for digital asset innovation.

data.title: Aave V3 Core Market Faces Liquidity Challenges After Kelp DAO Attack
data.description: Aave V3 Core Market on the Ethereum mainnet is experiencing significant liquidity issues following an attack on Kelp DAO. According to Foresight News, the USDT deposit size in the Aave V3 Core Market stands at $3.29 billion, with available liquidity reduced to approximately $400,000, resulting in a utilization rate of 99.99%. Meanwhile, the Plasma Market's USDT0 reserves amount to $1.35 billion, with available liquidity falling below $0.01, leading to a utilization rate of 100%.

data.title: Sky Suspends USDS Omnichain Bridge Functionality
data.description: Sky, formerly known as MakerDAO, announced on the X platform that it has temporarily suspended the cross-chain bridging functionality for USDS Omnichain Fungible Tokens. According to ChainCatcher, this decision comes as the company evaluates the impact of a security incident related to rsETH. Sky emphasized that its protocol and the USDS contract remain unaffected, maintaining full collateralization as per the design. Users can verify this status on the blockchain at any time.
